text.tspan: PROBLEM: with N servers, you map key -> hash(key) % N to pick a server.
Add or remove ONE server u2192 N changes u2192 nearly EVERY key moves u2192 cache stampede,
rebalance storm, painful cluster operations.

g.text: THE RING (hash space, e.g. 0 .. 2^64-1, wrapped into a circle)

text.tspan: ADDING server D (hash positions: 200, 900, 1600)
Only keys whose hash falls in:
A's old range that's now closer to D
B's old range that's now closer to D
C's old range that's now closer to D
... need to move. About K/N keys total, not K u00d7 (N-1)/N.
VIRTUAL NODES (vnodes) u2014 each physical server owns MANY ring positions
(typically 100u2013500). This:
u2022 SMOOTHS load distribution (one position would give very uneven shards)
u2022 lets you weight servers (bigger box = more vnodes)
u2022 spreads the 'moved keys' across many other servers (not just neighbors)
Server A
hash positions: 100, 800, 1500
Server B
hash positions: 300, 1000, 1700
Server C
hash positions: 500, 1200, 1900
Each KEY also hashes onto the ring.
Key is owned by the FIRST SERVER POSITION clockwise from key's hash.
